title: Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend Film's Teaser Reveals October 16 Release Date
content: Aniplex unveiled a new teaser trailer for Seishun Buta Yarō wa Dear Friend no Yume o Minai (Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend) film, the latest anime adaptation of Hajime Kamoshida's Rascal Does Not Dream (Seishun Buta Yarō or Aobuta) novel series, during its stage event for the film at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday. The teaser reveals the film's October 16 date. Image courtesy of Aniplex©2025 鴨志田 一/KADOKAWA/青ブタ ProjectAniplex also revealed a new version of the film's previously unveiled visual (shown right) which contains the release date.The film's returning cast includes:Kaito Ishikawa as Sakuta AzusagawaAsami Seto as Mai SakurajimaSora Amamiya as Uzuki HirokawaAya Yamane as Ikumi AkagiKonomi Kohara as Sara HimejiManaka Iwami as Miori MitōReina Ueda as Nene IwamizawaNao Tōyama as Tomoe KogaAtsumi Tanezaki as Rio FutabaMaaya Uchida as Nodoka ToyohamaYurika Kubo as Kaede AzusagawaInori Minase as Shōko MakinoharaSōichi Masui returns to direct the film at CloverWorks. Masahiro Yokotani is also back to oversee and write the script, and Satomi Tamura is again designing the characters. The band fox capture plan also returns to compose the music. Aniplex is once again distributing the film. The new film is teased as taking the story towards its finale. (The film shares the same name as the 15th and final novel volume.)Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai (Seishun Buta Yarō wa Bunny Girl-senpai no Yume wo Minai), the first television anime based on the light novel series, premiered in Japan in October 2018 and aired for 13 episodes. The anime adapts the story up to the fifth novel volume. Aniplex of America streamed the series on Crunchyroll, Funimation, and other services.Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl (Seishun Buta Yarō wa Yume-Miru Shōjo no Yume wo Minai), the anime film that adapts the the sixth and seventh light novel volumes in the franchise, opened in Japan in June 2019. Aniplex of America and Funimation Films screened the anime in the United States. Crunchyroll began streaming the film on June 19 with both English subtitles and dub.The first film Rascal Does Not Dream of a Sister Venturing Out opened in Japan in June 2023. The film sold 118,108 tickets in its first three days and earned 175,648,924 yen (about US$1.28 million) to rank #4 in its opening weekend. Rascal Does Not Dream of a Knapsack Kid, the second film, opened in Japan in December 2023, and ranked at #4 in its opening weekend. The film sold 119,000 tickets and earned 164,594,554 yen (about US$1.11 million) in its first three days.Rascal Does Not Dream of Santa Claus (Seishun Buta Yarō wa Santa Claus no Yume wo Minai), the television anime adapting the light novel series' "University Student Arc," premiered on July 5. Crunchyroll streamed the anime as it aired, and is also streaming an English dub.The original novel series has over 3 million copies in circulation. The Yen On imprint of Yen Press publishes the novel series, and Yen Press publishes Tsugumi Nanamiya's manga adaptation. Yen On will publish the Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend novel volume on January 6.Sources: AnimeJapan 2026 panel, press release, Disclosure: Kadokawa World Entertainment (KWE), a wholly owned subsidiary of Kadokawa Corporation, is the majority owner of Anime News Network, LLC. One or more of the companies mentioned in this article are part of the Kadokawa Group of Companies.
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: Aniplex unveiled a new teaser trailer for Seishun Buta Yarō wa Dear Friend no Yume o Minai ( Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend ) film, the latest anime adaptation of Hajime Kamoshida 's Rascal Does Not Dream ( Seishun Buta Yarō or Aobuta) novel series, during its stage event for the film at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday. The teaser reveals the film's October 16 date.
Aniplex also revealed a new version of the film's previously unveiled visual (shown right) which contains the release date.
The film's returning cast includes:
- Kaito Ishikawa as Sakuta Azusagawa
- Asami Seto as Mai Sakurajima
- Sora Amamiya as Uzuki Hirokawa
- Aya Yamane as Ikumi Akagi
- Konomi Kohara as Sara Himeji
- Manaka Iwami as Miori Mitō
- Reina Ueda as Nene Iwamizawa
- Nao Tōyama as Tomoe Koga
- Atsumi Tanezaki as Rio Futaba
- Maaya Uchida as Nodoka Toyohama
- Yurika Kubo as Kaede Azusagawa
- Inori Minase as Shōko Makinohara

Sōichi Masui returns to direct the film at CloverWorks . Masahiro Yokotani is also back to oversee and write the script, and Satomi Tamura is again designing the characters. The band fox capture plan also returns to compose the music. Aniplex is once again distributing the film.
The new film is teased as taking the story towards its finale. (The film shares the same name as the 15th and final novel volume.)

Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai ( Seishun Buta Yarō wa Bunny Girl-senpai no Yume wo Minai ), the first television anime based on the light novel series, premiered in Japan in October 2018 and aired for 13 episodes. The anime adapts the story up to the fifth novel volume. Aniplex of America streamed the series on Crunchyroll , Funimation , and other services.

Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl ( Seishun Buta Yarō wa Yume-Miru Shōjo no Yume wo Minai ), the anime film that adapts the the sixth and seventh light novel volumes in the franchise , opened in Japan in June 2019. Aniplex of America and Funimation Films screened the anime in the United States. Crunchyroll began streaming the film on June 19 with both English subtitles and dub .

The first film Rascal Does Not Dream of a Sister Venturing Out opened in Japan in June 2023. The film sold 118,108 tickets in its first three days and earned 175,648,924 yen (about US$1.28 million) to rank #4 in its opening weekend. Rascal Does Not Dream of a Knapsack Kid , the second film, opened in Japan in December 2023, and ranked at #4 in its opening weekend. The film sold 119,000 tickets and earned 164,594,554 yen (about US$1.11 million) in its first three days.

Rascal Does Not Dream of Santa Claus ( Seishun Buta Yarō wa Santa Claus no Yume wo Minai ), the television anime adapting the light novel series' "University Student Arc," premiered on July 5. Crunchyroll streamed the anime as it aired, and is also streaming an English dub .

The original novel series has over 3 million copies in circulation. The Yen On imprint of Yen Press publishes the novel series, and Yen Press publishes Tsugumi Nanamiya 's manga adaptation. Yen On will publish the Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend novel volume on January 6.

title: 'Gensou Suikoden' Announces Supporting Cast
content:
The Gensou Suikoden ( Suikoden: The Anime ) White Stage at AnimeJapan 2026 revealed supporting cast members and a fourth key visual (pictured) on Sunday. The anime series adapting Konami's fantasy role-playing game will begin airing in October 2026.

Cast Culgan: Jun Kasama ( Revenger ) Seed: Yoshitsugu Matsuoka ( Re:Zero kara Hajimeru Isekai Seikatsu ) Leknaat: Megumi Han ( Tate no Yuusha no Nariagari ) Gengen: Shouta Hayama ( Tokyo Revengers ) Rikimaru: Ryuunosuke Watanuki ( Blue Lock ) Rina: Saya Hitomi ( Chi. Chikyuu no Undou ni Tsuite ) Eilie: Umino Mizutama Bolgan: Yuusuke Sasaki ( Dandadan )

Yuuzou Satou ( Exception ) is helming the anime at Konami animation . Michihiro Tsuchiya ( Major 2nd 2nd Season ) is handling the series composition and Ryou Yamauchi ( Uchi no Shishou wa Shippo ga Nai ) is designing the characters. Kouji Nakamura ( Eureka Seven AO ) is composing the music.

The Suikoden franchise debuted in 1995 on the PlayStation, drawing inspiration from the classic Chinese novel Shui Hu Zhuan ( Water Margin ). Konami released Gensou Suikoden II ( Suikoden II ) in December 1998, followed by three more games in the main series in July 2002, August 2004, and February 2006 as well as multiple spin-offs.

Shinjirou Hori penned a light novel adaptation under Kadokawa's Dengeki Bunko imprint with illustrations by Fumi Ishikawa from December 2000 to September 2001.
author: MyAnimeList

title: 'Rakudai Kenja no Gakuin Musou' Announces Additional Cast, Teaser Promo
content:
The Rakudai Kenja no Gakuin Musou: Nidome no Tensei, S-Rank Cheat Majutsushi Boukenroku ( The Failed Sage's Academy Domination ) AnimeJapan 2026 stage event at the NBCUniversal booth revealed additional cast and a teaser promotional video on Sunday. The anime series adapting Arata Shiraishi and Kentarou 's fantasy adventure manga will begin airing on Tokyo MX, ABC TV, BS Fuji, WOWOW in July 2026.

Cast Ephtal (Sage/Old Man): Hiroki Touchi ( Sousou no Frieren ) Ephtal (Childhood): Ayaka Fukuhara ( Kawaii dake ja Nai Shikimori-san ) Ilya: Emiri Katou ( Overlord ) Fraser: Daisuke Hirakawa ( Ore dake Level Up na Ken )

Hisashi Ishii ( Yuusha, Yamemasu ) is directing the anime at EMT Squared . Deko Akao ( Saigo ni Hitotsu dake Onegai shitemo Yoroshii deshou ka ) is handling the series composition, and Hideki Furukawa ( Strike the Blood III ) is designing the characters.

Shiraishi began writing the adventure fantasy story on the Shousetsuka ni Narou website in 2018. Kadokawa published the light novel with illustrations by Denim Uo under the Sneaker Bunko imprint in eight volumes from October 2019 to March 2022.

The manga adaptation by Kentarou began its serialization on the Manga UP! website in September 2019. Square Enix released the eighth volume in June 2025.

Square Enix publishes the manga in English on the Manga UP! and Comikey websites.

Teaser PV

title: 'Heroine? Saint? No, I'm an All-Works Maid (And Proud of It)!' TV Anime Reveals July Debut, More Cast
content: The official website for the television anime of Atekichi and Yukiko's Heroine? Saint? No, I'm an All-Works Maid (And Proud of It)! (Heroine? Seijo? Iie, All Works Maid desu (ko)!) light novel series revealed a teaser promotional video and one more cast member on Sunday. The staff also revealed the anime will debut in July on Tokyo MX and BS Fuji, and will also stream on d Anime Store. The new cast member is Yōko Hikasa as Anna-Marie Victirium.Image via Heroine? Saint? No, I'm an All-Works Maid (And Proud of It)! anime's website©あてきち・TOブックス／オールワークスメイド製作委員会The anime will star: Yume Miyamoto as Melody Wave Rumi Ōkubo as Luciana Rudleberg Kōhei Amasaki will voice Christopher Von Théolas EMT Squared is producing the anime.Seven Seas licensed and releases both the original novels and the manga adaptation. The company describes the story: "I will become the world's most wonderful maid!" With her skirt fluttering in black and white, the girl shouted. Her name is Melody. A reincarnated former Japanese girl, she now pursues her dream in her new life, working as an all-purpose maid for a poor count's family in the kingdom of Théolas. When she makes it, even cheap tea turns into a luxury brew, and a dilapidated mansion is quickly restored to new! Cleaning, serving, hunting, DIY—leave it all to her and her powerful magic. Unbeknownst to Melody, this world is actually an otome game, and she is the most powerful and invincible heroine, the saint! Yet, she remains oblivious to this fact. Romance with handsome men? Attacks by the Demon Lord? Work comes first! A fantasy of misunderstood work that unknowingly shatters destiny!Atekichi launched the ongoing light novel series on the "Shōsetsuka ni Narō" website in February 2017. TO Books started publishing the novels with illustration by Yukiko in March 2020. Keiko launched the manga adaptation of the series on TO Books' Corona EX website in March 2022. The light novels' ninth volume will ship on April 1. The manga's seventh volume will ship on July 1.Source: Heroine? Saint? No, I'm an All-Works Maid (And Proud of It)! anime's website via Ota-Suke
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: The official website for the television anime of Atekichi and Yukiko 's Heroine? Saint? No, I'm an All-Works Maid (And Proud of It)! ( Heroine? Seijo? Iie, All Works Maid desu (ko)! ) light novel series revealed a teaser promotional video and one more cast member on Sunday. The staff also revealed the anime will debut in July on Tokyo MX and BS Fuji , and will also stream on d Anime Store .
The new cast member is Yōko Hikasa as Anna-Marie Victirium.

contentBlocks.credit: Image via Heroine? Saint? No, I'm an All-Works Maid (And Proud of It)! anime's website

contentBlocks.text: The anime will star:
- Yume Miyamoto as Melody Wave
- Rumi Ōkubo as Luciana Rudleberg
- Kōhei Amasaki will voice Christopher Von Théolas
EMT Squared is producing the anime.
Seven Seas licensed and releases both the original novels and the manga adaptation. The company describes the story:

"I will become the world's most wonderful maid!" With her skirt fluttering in black and white, the girl shouted. Her name is Melody. A reincarnated former Japanese girl, she now pursues her dream in her new life, working as an all-purpose maid for a poor count's family in the kingdom of Théolas. When she makes it, even cheap tea turns into a luxury brew, and a dilapidated mansion is quickly restored to new! Cleaning, serving, hunting, DIY—leave it all to her and her powerful magic. Unbeknownst to Melody, this world is actually an otome game, and she is the most powerful and invincible heroine, the saint! Yet, she remains oblivious to this fact. Romance with handsome men? Attacks by the Demon Lord? Work comes first! A fantasy of misunderstood work that unknowingly shatters destiny!

Atekichi launched the ongoing light novel series on the "Shōsetsuka ni Narō" website in February 2017. TO Books started publishing the novels with illustration by Yukiko in March 2020. Keiko launched the manga adaptation of the series on TO Books ' Corona EX website in March 2022. The light novels' ninth volume will ship on April 1. The manga's seventh volume will ship on July 1.

title: 'Baki-dou' Second Part Announced
content:
The Baki-dou Blue Stage at AnimeJapan 2026 announced a second part on Sunday, revealing a teaser visual (pictured) and teaser promotional video. The second part will be released worldwide exclusively on Netflix.

TMS Entertainment adapted the Hanma Baki prequel manga into two anime seasons on Netflix—the first season debuted with 12 episodes in September 2021 and the second season streamed in 27 episodes from July to August in 2023.

A crossover anime movie between the Hanma Baki and Kengan Ashura anime premiered worldwide on Netflix in June 2024. The first part of Baki-dou premiered on Netflix on February 26.

Keisuke Itagaki began serializing Baki-dou , the fourth main entry in the martial arts manga series, in Shounen Champion in March 2014 and ended it in April 2018.

A sequel manga, also titled Baki-dou ( Baki-dou 2 ), ran in the same magazine from October 2018 to June 2023, followed by Baki Rahen , which began in August 2023. The sixth volume was published on February 6.

Teaser PV

Baki-dou Part 2 on MAL
author: MyAnimeList

title: 'Isekai Nonbiri Nouka 2' Reveals Additional Cast, Second Promo
content:
• The Isekai Nonbiri Nouka 2 ( Farming Life in Another World Season 2 ) AnimeJapan 2026 stage event at the Pony Canyon booth revealed additional cast, a key visual (pictured right), and a second promotional video on Sunday. The second season adapting Kinosuke Naitou 's fantasy slice of life light novel will air at 10.30 p.m. on AT-X followed by, TV Tokyo, BS TV Tokyo, and Tulip TV on April 6.

Cast Majin (The Demon God): Hikaru Midorikawa ( Black Clover ) Jack: Rikuya Yasuda ( Beelzebub-jou no Okinimesu mama. ) Morte: Eri Yukimura ( Blue Lock ) Bell Forgma: Mariko Honda ( Kami no Tou ) Gou Forgma: Yasuhiro Mamiya ( Vigilante: Boku no Hero Academia Illegals ) Kuzden: Fumiya Imai ( Given )

Ryouichi Kuraya ( Tsugumomo ) is directing the anime and handling the series composition and script at Zero-G . Yoshiko Saitou ( Kubo-san wa Mob wo Yurusanai ) is designing the characters, with Johannes Nilsson ( Kaifuku Jutsushi no Yarinaoshi ) (Team-MAX) and Yasuharu Takanashi ( Tsuki ga Michibiku Isekai Douchuu ) (Team-MAX) composing the music.

Produced by Zero-G, the first season aired in Winter 2023. HIDIVE streamed the anime with subtitles and an English dub.

Naitou began penning the novel on the Shousetsuka ni Narou website in December 2016. Kadokawa began publishing the novel with illustrations by Yasumo in October 2017 and released the 20th volume on February 28. Isekai Nonbiri Nouka has a cumulative 5.7 million copies of its volumes in circulation.

Yasuyuki Tsurugi began drawing a manga adaptation on the ComicWalker website in October 2017. Kadokawa published the 16th volume on March 9.

One Peace Books licensed the manga in English in September 2020 and published the 11th volume in November 2025, with the 12th scheduled to be released on June 23.

PV 2
author: MyAnimeList

title: 'Let's Go Kaiki-gumi' Reveals Additional Cast, Staff, Teaser Promo for Summer 2026
content:
The Let's Go Kaiki-gumi presentation on the TBS stage at AnimeJapan 2026 revealed additional cast, staff, ending theme, key visual (pictured), and a teaser promotional video on Sunday. The anime adapting Byuu 's supernatural comedy manga will begin airing on July 5 at 4:30 p.m. on TBS and 28 affiliated channels.

Cast Shurako: Yoshino Aoyama ( Mikadono Sanshimai wa Angai, Choroi. ) Doppelganger: Yuuki Sakakihara ( Dead Mount Death Play ) Noroi no Ningyou: Mayumi Shintani ( FLCL )

Staff Color Design: Taeko Mizuno ( Ragna Crimson ) Art Director: Yoshimi Umino ( Hotel Inhumans ) Director of Photography: Misaki Horuichi ( Starmyu ) Sound Director: Kouhei Yoshida ( Dark Gathering ) Sound Production: Half H.P Studio Music: Akiyuki Tateyama ( Nige Jouzu no Wakagimi )

Writer Uketsu is performing the ending theme.

Yutaka Hirata ( Golden Kamuy episode director) is helming the anime at C-Station . Mutsumi Itou ( Heya Camp△ ) is handling the series composition and penning the script, while Yoshimitsu Yamashita ( Samurai Flamenco ) is designing the characters.

Byuu serialized the supernatural comedy manga on the Omocoro website from July 2018 to July 2024. Shogakukan published the fifth volume in August 2024.

PV
author: MyAnimeList

title: 'Heroine? Seijo? Iie, All Works Maid desu (Hokori)!' Reveals Production Staff, Additional Cast, Teaser Promo for Summer 2026
content:
The Heroine? Seijo? Iie, All Works Maid desu (Hokori)! ( Heroine? Saint? No, I'm an All-Works Maid (And Proud of It)! ) AnimeJapan 2026 stage event at the d Anime Store booth revealed the production staff, additional cast and a teaser promotional video on Sunday.

Produced by EMT Squared , the anime series adapting Atekichi 's isekai fantasy light novel air on Tokyo MX and BS Fuji beginning in July 2026.

Voice actress Youko Hikasa ( Summertime Render ) is joining the cast as Anne-Marie Victilium.

Staff Chief Director: Shinji Ishihira ( Edens Zero ) Director: Naoya Murakawa ( Yuusha Party wo Tsuihou sareta Beast Tamer, Saikyoushu no Nekomimi Shoujo to Deau episode director) Series Composition: Toshizou Nemoto ( Log Horizon ) Character Design: Eri Tokugawa ( Tensei Kizoku no Isekai Boukenroku: Jichou wo Shiranai Kamigami no Shito ) Sub-character Design: Eri Kojima ( Kami-tachi ni Hirowareta Otoko ) Art Director: Minoru Ookouchi ( Potion-danomi de Ikinobimasu! ) Color Design: Mika Iwami ( Kuma Kuma Kuma Bear ) Director of Photography: Atsushi Iwasaki ( Fairy Tail ) Editing: Honami Yamagishi ( Lv2 kara Cheat datta Motoyuusha Kouho no Mattari Isekai Life ) Music: Ken Itou ( 100-man no Inochi no Ue ni Ore wa Tatteiru ) Music Production: Lantis Sound Director: Hiroto Morishita ( Seirei Gensouki ) Sound Production: DAX Production

Atekichi began penning the novel on the Shousetsuka ni Narou website in February 2017. TO Books began publishing the series with illustrations by Yukiko in March 2020. The eighth volume went on sale in October 2025, with the ninth volume set for an April 1 release. The light novel has a cumulative 600,000 copies of its volumes in print.

Keiko originally began serializing a manga adaptation on the Piccoma website in March 2021 before transferring it to Comic Corona in May that year. TO Books plans to release the seventh volume on July 1.

Seven Seas Entertainment licensed both the light novel and manga in English in June 2024. The fifth novel volume was shipped on February 3, with the sixth scheduled to be released on June 9. The fifth manga volume was shipped on January 6, with the sixth scheduled to be released on May 5.

Teaser PV
author: MyAnimeList

Staff Chief Director: Shinji Ishihira ( Edens Zero ) Director: Naoya Murakawa ( Yuusha Party wo Tsuihou sareta Beast Tamer, Saikyoushu no Nekomimi Shoujo to Deau episode director) Series Composition: Toshizou Nemoto ( Log Horizon ) Character Design: Eri Tokugawa ( Tensei Kizoku no Isekai Boukenroku: Jichou wo Shiranai Kamigami no Shito ) Sub-character Design: Eri Kojima ( Kami-tachi ni Hirowareta Otoko ) Art Director: Minoru Ookouchi ( Potion-danomi de Ikinobimasu! ) Color Design: Mika Iwami ( Kuma Kuma Kuma Bear ) Director of Photography: Atsushi Iwasaki ( Fairy Tail ) Editing: Honami Yamagishi ( Lv2 kara Cheat datta Motoyuusha Kouho no Mattari Isekai Life ) Music: Ken Itou ( 100-man no Inochi no Ue ni Ore wa Tatteiru ) Music Production: Lantis Sound Director: Hiroto Morishita ( Seirei Gensouki ) Sound Production: DAX Production

Atekichi began penning the novel on the Shousetsuka ni Narou website in February 2017. TO Books began publishing the series with illustrations by Yukiko in March 2020. The eighth volume went on sale in October 2025, with the ninth volume set for an April 1 release. The light novel has a cumulative 600,000 copies of its volumes in print.

Keiko originally began serializing a manga adaptation on the Piccoma website in March 2021 before transferring it to Comic Corona in May that year. TO Books plans to release the seventh volume on July 1.

Seven Seas Entertainment licensed both the light novel and manga in English in June 2024. The fifth novel volume was shipped on February 3, with the sixth scheduled to be released on June 9. The fifth manga volume was shipped on January 6, with the sixth scheduled to be released on May 5.
Teaser PV

title: 'Seihantai na Kimi to Boku' Season 2 Announced for Summer 2026
content:
The Seihantai na Kimi to Boku ( You and I Are Polar Opposites ) White Stage announcement on Sunday that a second season is in production, revealing an announcement video. The second season is scheduled to premiere on July 5 at 5:00 p.m. on MBS and TBS.

Produced by Lapin Track , the television anime adapting Koucha Agasawa 's school romcom manga began airing on January 11. Crunchyroll simulcasted the anime with multiple subtitles and an English dub.

Agasawa originally drew the romantic comedy manga on the Shounen Jump+ web magazine as a one-shot in January 2021 before beginning regular serialization in May 2022. The manga ended its over 3.5 year-run with 65 chapters in November 2024. Shueisha published the eighth and final volume in March 2025.

Shueisha's Manga Plus platform began publishing the chapters in English in June 2022. VIZ Media licensed the manga in English in October 2023 and published the eighth and final volume on February 3.

Announcement

Seihantai na Kimi to Boku 2nd Season on MAL

Produced by Lapin Track , the television anime adapting Koucha Agasawa 's school romcom manga began airing on January 11. Crunchyroll simulcasted the anime with multiple subtitles and an English dub.

Agasawa originally drew the romantic comedy manga on the Shounen Jump+ web magazine as a one-shot in January 2021 before beginning regular serialization in May 2022. The manga ended its over 3.5 year-run with 65 chapters in November 2024. Shueisha published the eighth and final volume in March 2025.

title: 'Romelia Senki' Unveils Additional Cast, 2 Cours Broadcast
content:
The Pony Canyon booth at AnimeJapan 2026 revealed four additional cast and a second teaser visual (pictured) for the Romelia Senki ( Romelia War Chronicle ) television anime on Sunday. The anime series adapting Ryou Ariyama 's action fantasy light novel will air in two cours in 2026.

Cast Henri: Yuuya Hirose ( Mato Seihei no Slave ) Elisabeth: Sayaka Senbongi ( Tensei shitara Slime Datta Ken ) Ekaterina: Mariya Ise ( JoJo no Kimyou na Bouken Part 6: Stone Ocean ) Ryoki: Asaka ( Around 40 Otoko no Isekai Tsuuhan )

Hiroshi Shirai ( Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero episode director) is helming the anime at Atra . Aya Satsuki ( Kawaisugi Crisis ) is handling the series composition and penning the script. Yoshiko Saitou ( Towa no Yuugure ) and Yuuta Ootaka ( Re:Zero kara Hajimeru Isekai Seikatsu 3rd Season animation director) is designing the characters. KOHTA YAMAMOTO ( Dark Gathering ) is composing the music.

Ariyama originally began penning the action fantasy novel on the Shousetsuka ni Narou website in August 2019. Shogakukan began printing the title under its Gagaga Books label in June 2020, featuring illustrations by Kodama . The sixth volume shipped in November 2025.

Ryou Kamito began drawing a manga adaptation on the Manga Doa app in April 2021. Mag Garden released the fifth volume in November 2025.

Seven Seas Entertainment licensed the manga in English in March 2025. The first volume was published on January 13, with the second volume scheduled for release on April 27.
